primary_diagnosis.name: Severe Raynaud's phenomenon attack in limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis (CREST); critical digital ischaemia not fully excludable remotely

comorbidities.name: Limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis (CREST, anti-centromere positive)

pathophysiology_summary: Raynaud's phenomenon is episodic digital vasospasm producing the triphasic colour change (white/ischaemia -> blue/cyanosis -> red/reperfusion), triggered by cold. In limited systemic sclerosis (CREST) it is secondary and can be severe and prolonged, with a risk of digital ulceration and, rarely, critical ischaemia. The reassuring features here are bilateral symmetry, a clear cold trigger, and colour recovering with warming (against fixed arterial occlusion/embolism, which is typically unilateral with absent pulses). However, telehealth cannot examine pulses, capillary refill or perform Allen's test, so critical digital ischaemia cannot be fully excluded if warming fails. Safe management is guided core rewarming with reassessment, urgent GP review for a vasodilator (e.g. nifedipine), and a firm digital-ischaemia safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Cold-triggered bilateral symmetric triphasic colour change with a known Raynaud's/CREST history.
Must exclude fixed ischaemia (usually unilateral, non-recovering, absent pulses); recovery with warming argues against it but pulses cannot be checked remotely.
A digit fixed white/blue/black despite warming would signal critical ischaemia needing emergency care.
No skin breakdown, pus or fluctuance.
Colour recovering with warming (DI-001), bilateral symmetric cold-triggered attack with no fixed ischaemia/gangrene (DI-002) — a severe but reversing vasospastic attack.
Cannot be fully excluded without examination if warming fails; retained as the escalation trigger.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: guide immediate core rewarming and reassess; if the attack reverses, urgent GP review for a vasodilator with a digital-ischaemia safety-net; escalate to ED if any digit stays white/blue/numb despite warming or turns black. Reassuring without a rewarming trial/safety-net, or ignoring fixed ischaemia, are both errors.

red_flags.description: Fixed digital ischaemia — a digit remaining white/blue/numb despite warming, or turning black (critical ischaemia/gangrene)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Critical digital ischaemia threatens the digit and needs emergency vascular/rheumatology care.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delayed treatment of critical ischaemia leads to digital gangrene/amputation.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Telling the patient it's 'just Raynaud's' without guiding core rewarming, reassessing, or safety-netting fixed ischaemia.
Dispatching an ambulance for a bilateral symmetric attack that is already recovering with warming, when guided rewarming and urgent GP review suffice.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Guide core rewarming and reassess; urgent GP review for a vasodilator; ED immediately if any digit stays white/blue/numb despite warming or turns black.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Reassuring a fixed-ischaemia digit as 'just Raynaud's' risks gangrene; over-triaging a reversing attack wastes emergency resources.
